idea: cuBLAS reference baseline
1p2c split-output WN=128 STAGES=2 non-persistent fp16->fp32; first functional baseline at 198.6 TFLOPS
expected_gain: establish baseline, expect 300+ TFLOPS with optimization

idea: Switched from 1p2c to 1p1c WN=256 STAGES=3: reduced CTA threads from 384 to 256, enabling 2+ CTAs/SM vs prior 1 CTA/SM at 128 regs/thread
Persistent 1p1c WN=256 STAGES=4 with 114 CTAs: only 114 parallel CTAs serializes 2352 tiles each per CTA, causing massive execution serialization vs non-persistent 16384 CTAs
1p1c WN=256 STAGES=3 maxrregcount=160: same as iter002 at 291 TFLOPS, register spilling has no benefit
1p1c WN=256 STAGES=3 padded output SMEM: same result 292T, padding did not reduce bank conflicts since Choreo already handles layout. Root issue is SMEM at 184KB prevents 2 CTAs/SM
1p1c WN=128 STAGES=3 hoped for 2 CTAs/SM via SMEM reduction: got worse at 206T vs 291T at WN=256. Halving WN halves arithmetic intensity per CTA, losing more than we gain from extra CTAs
1p1c WN=192 STAGES=2: 196T, even worse than WN=256. Narrower tile consistently loses. Must stay at WN=256 and fix latency differently
1p1c WN=256 STAGES=4: increased stages from 3 to 4, slight improvement 291T->293T. More prefetch depth slightly reduces CTA barrier wait. SMEM=224KB barely fits in 228KB
1p2c split-output WN=256 TM=128 STAGES=2 FP32: verification fails with all-zero output, suspected Choreo codegen issue with 2 consumer warpgroups and FP32 TMA output SMEM size 64KB box

1p3c WN=128 TM=192 STAGES=3: verified correct but 218.7T vs iter008 293.3T; 512-thread block requires maxrregcount=128 causing spilling; lower WN=128 reduces arithmetic intensity; structural change confirmed: sync tma.copy works for output but registers are the bottleneck
1p1c WN=128 STAGES=3 for 2 CTAs/SM: 104KB SMEM. Lower AI hurt more than occupancy gain.
1p1c WN=128 STAGES=3 for 2 CTAs/SM: 104KB SMEM. Lower AI hurt more than occupancy gain.
WN=256 STAGES=4 1p1c + --use-warpspec --hoist-offset: explicit warpgroup barriers + address hoist
NCU R12: same metrics as iter008. Barrier stall 3.75 (TMA 3x slower than WGMMA). Next: manual CU optimization to overlap WGMMA with TMA refill.
tma-cluster-aware: uses cluster TMA mbarrier. No cluster launch config -> cluster_size=1. Less efficient than standard cp.async.bulk.
cluster TMA (tma-cluster-aware) without cluster_size launch config: cluster_size=1 degrades vs standard cp.async.bulk
Manual .cu: L2_PROMOTION_L2_256B for both lhs and rhs TMA tensor maps. B tile 32KB stays in L2 across multiple CTAs reading same N-strip.
NCU R14: L2 promotion worked - LTS 68->75%, DRAM 60.9->60.3%. Barrier still 3.75. Next: maxrregcount=256 and cluster TMA.
warpgroup_arrive() moved before iv_warp loop + L2_256B. No measurable improvement. warpgroup_arrive() position doesnt matter.
maxrregcount=256 ptxas ignores it - unconstrained regs cause spilling. Worse 147T vs 299T.
maxrregcount=256 ignored by ptxas: unconstrained regs -> severe spilling, 147T
2x K-loop unroll: process 2 K-tiles per iteration, issue both WGMMA batches before waiting. Overlaps tensor core pipeline. +12T vs iter014.
2x K-unroll: 311.8T. Next: try depth-2 WGMMA pipeline with warpgroup_wait<1>.
4x K-unroll: waits for 4 stages before computing, causing 4x initial stall. 172T.
